title: Jan Averkin
text:
Welcome! I’m a doctoral researcher in economics at ETH Zürich. My work sits at the intersection of development economics, social protection, and the economics of child labour, with a regional focus on West Africa.
I study how households in low-income, agricultural settings respond to policy — in particular how cash transfers reshape children’s work, schooling, and household decision-making. My current research evaluates the effects of an unconditional cash transfer on child labour in cocoa-farming households in Ghana.
Beyond research, I occasionally write here about things I’m working on, reading, or building. Take a look at the Research page for an overview of my projects, the Blog for posts, or my CV.






This site is a work in progress — content is still being filled in.

title: Hello, world
text:
This is the first post on my new site. I plan to use this space for short notes on my research, things I’m reading, and the occasional project or side interest.
Because the site is built with Quarto, posts can mix prose with live code. By default this post renders as plain Markdown, so the R below is shown but not executed. To make a post actually run R and embed the output, set engine: knitr in its front matter and render locally (you have R installed):
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, colour = class)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal()
To add another post, copy the posts/welcome/ folder, rename it, and edit the front matter (title, date, categories). The Blog page picks it up automatically.
